summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud config to hardcoded endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js harvests installer-side identity and cloud-context data and POSTs it as JSON to a hardcoded author-controlled endpoint at https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Collected fields include: git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and repo.git/config, recent committer emails from git reflog, GitHub CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key email comments from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, hostname, username, USERDOMAIN, DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf, cwd, and CI provider. The package presents itself as 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK' and declares a peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk, but ships no dist/index.js (the declared main entry) — the advertised library is a lure and the postinstall script is the sole effect of installing this package. Repository URL points to a non-existent org. The behavior is install-time bulk exfiltration of developer/cloud identity material that allows the receiver to correlate cloud accounts, source-repo authorship, and SSH identities to specific developers for follow-on attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
